1

15
16

18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Man Tax: Alex Fine
The older I grow, the more I listen to people who don't talk much. -Germain G. Glien So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.description: Looking for Better Privacy? Not all apps collect the same amount of data. If privacy matters to you, consider privacy-focused alternatives like Signal for messaging, Brave for browsing, and Proton Mail for email. The best choice depends on your needs and how you use your devices. Question for everyone: Which app have you switched for better privacy, or which privacy-focused app would you recommend?

item.title: RIP Nick Dale
item.description: https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/helicopter-pilot-killed-wildfire-colorado-9.7269241 https://skiesmag.com/news/bc-helicopter-pilot-killed-in-colorado-wildfire-crash/ These shitstains are demanding that smoke from the 200 fires buring in northern Ontario “not be allowed” to cross the border into the U.S.: Claudia Tenney (R-NY), John James (R-MI), Jack Bergman (R-MI), John Moolenaar (R-MI), and Lisa McClain (R-MI). tRump complained that the smoke is “unnecessarily invading” the U.S. and is going to add new 50% tariffs on Canadian goods. Smoke from U.S. wildfires frequently “invades” Canada, too. We don’t bitch or make threats, we help you fight the fires and one of our pilots died helping you. Your air and water pollution cross the border, too, as did a lot of ash from Mount St Helens. When ice storms or hurricanes destroy your power grids, we send crews to help restore them. Do not ever wonder why a majority of Canadians now see the U.S. as an enemy. Not just an annoyance. Not just a problem. An enemy. Ponder that reality.

item.title: The Trump admin's case against the former Olympic canoeist accused of damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is about to fall apart. They failed to preserve the physical evidence against him–the lining of the pool that he is accused of damaging.
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: We are not okay
item.description: I joined the military in 2008, my junior year of high school. I shipped out the next year. My stepmother and father were going through a divorce, leaving me without any resources, family, or anything. Quite literally was booted out of my house three months before graduation.My father wanted me to have to struggle on my own before the military. He wanted to see if I could survive his words, not mine. Years later, I got out of the army and found everything in shambles.The economy hadn't gotten any better. The government was constantly shut down and having been former military intelligence around now 2016, Donald Trump had started destroying every career opportunity that I possibly had. I'd gotten a network security degree by now, couldn't find work, could barely afford a horrible apartment in the least expensive place I could find. We've been at a constant state of war and the world has continuously gotten worse. I have access to the Veteran Affairs Loan before housing and even that, I still can't find housing. I don't have hope. I'm never going to get to enjoy my life. I'm heading into my 40s, still struggling like I was 10 years ago. I have to constantly ask people for help just to feed myself or keep the bills paid enough to keep the lights on. I'm now a disabled veteran, miserable, With no hope.
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.title: Ah well

item.title: ICC
item.dc:creator: Resistance1000

item.title: “Go Ask This Old Mormon White Lady About Why Acid and Weed Will Ruin Your Life”
item.description:
Today a random book that I read in high school popped into my head: “Go Ask Alice.” It was presented as a real diary from a teenage girl who fell down the path of drug addiction and ended up dying from it. Diary-entry stories are one of my favorite genres, and the thought that this was someone’s real diary was especially compelling. It was… interesting. And awful. I wasn’t really sure what to make of this anonymous author, and I sorta put the story in the back of my head. I looked it up again today out of curiosity, to find that it wasn’t actually written by a real teenager who died from drug addiction, it was secretly written by a child psychologist named Dr. Beatrice Sparks as a cautionary tale to other impressionable teens out there. Then it turns out that “Dr. Sparks” wasn’t really a psychologist. She was just kinda/sorta one because she was a Mormon housewife that sometimes helped out at a Mormon summer camp for troubled teenagers, and that’s when she met this fictional “Alice.” Rick Emerson wrote a book about the entire ordeal: “Unmask Alice: LSD, Satanic Panic, and the Imposter Behind the World's Most Notorious Diaries” Beatrice pulled this stunt with a few other “anonymous diaries”, including one from a reformed gang member that dabbled in satanism! Because everyone knows that the thug life includes sacrificing a holy virgin to the dark lord Lucifer! . Looking back at the book with this context, it actually makes a lot of sense. Lots of: “Oh, diary! It was the most wonderful day! I love God, I really do!” And the way she describes a gay character in there as a degenerate drug dealer who was pretending to be straight to prostitute young girls for drugs. My personal favorite is the entry January 24.
I could not believe what I just read.
Quick Recap: I was once studying to be a research neuropsychologist, which meant that I was focused heavily on the neuroscience part to psychology. . . WHAT IS A “REWARD PATHWAY?” Your brain has an entire system of pathways and neural networks that carry out various functions. One thing that has been found essential to our survival as a species is to make certain aspects of survival feel good so that we want to do them. Good food, sex, relaxation when you’re stressed/tired, drinking water when you’re thirsty - all of these things feel good because it secures our survival as individuals and as a species. The main brain chemical responsible for this is called “dopamine,” which is the chemical that makes us feel pleasure. Highly addictive drugs overstimulate that reward pathway, causing large influxes of dopamine (though how that works depends on the drug). This makes it feel really, REALLY, UNIMAGINABLY GOOD. And just like how we want to keep doing these essential survival things that feel good, we want to do the drugs that feel good. Heroin, cocaine, and methamphetamines all target the reward pathway specifically, which is why you keep wanting to do it. The drug tricks your brain into rewarding you for taking it, and thus you want to keep taking more. Marijuana and psychedelics don’t trigger the reward pathway like that. Marijuana is generally safe to use, but it is first and foremost a psychoactive substance, which means that it alters the way your brain normally regulates its brain chemicals. Some people’s brains develop an addiction to this effect. So yes, marijuana has a low potential to be addictive to certain people, especially if they already have addictive personalities. That doesn’t mean that marijuana is so dangerous that it will definitely destroy your life with a crippling addiction. . . PSYCHEDELICS NEED TO BE USED CAUTIOUSLY BECAUSE THEY CAN TAKE A TOLL ON YOUR BRAIN AND KIDNEYS! The overall danger of psychedelics is a hotly debated topic, but it’s simply a fact that you cannot use it all the time. Psychedelics can be hard on your brain because they pack a wallop to another brain chemical called “serotonin,” which is the chemical responsible for feeling happiness. It causes your brain to be submerged with serotonin. These effects are intense, and sometimes unpredictable. The other part of this is that psychedelics can impact your kidneys - it’s uncommon, but it happens. There have been cases of patients with acute kidney injury due to constant use of magic mushrooms. You shouldn’t be going on acid benders every day because both your brain and your kidneys cannot handle that. It’s a “take in moderation with good safety precaution” type of drug. UNLESS YOU’RE ON THE SCHIZOPHRENIA SPECTRUM OR HAVE OTHER PSYCHOTIC SYMPTOMS - THEN NEVER TAKE IT!! There has been some research to suggest that psychedelics can potentially be used to treat psychotic disorders, but that is still only ongoing research. Don’t take drugs that add to your hallucinations.
I laughed because this was so completely ridiculous, but part of that laughter is in anger. This stunt wasn’t just inappropriate. It was fraudulent, conniving, and a woefully inaccurate representation of how drug addiction destroys lives. Some are saying that she had “good intentions,” but I don’t agree. I don’t think that her intentions were truly good. I think she was subtly pushing an agenda, and using one of the most vulnerable groups of people to do that. It wasn’t a story about how drugs steal lives, it was a story about how “straying from God” destroyed her life - with a heavy emphasis on things that Mormons view as debauchery. One of the articles about it from University of Southern Queensland summed this up so beautifully: “Of course, in time, it is not uncommon for authors or publishers to confront charges of deception in their books. Sparks, however, never confessed to her literary crimes. She died in 2012, at the age of 95. Unlike her teen diarists, whose lives were cut tragically short, Sparks’s life, it seems, was as long as the lies she told.” This book is better used as kindling for your next campfire.
University of Southern Queensland Article: https://www.unisq.edu.au/news/2024/12/the-conversation-go-ask-alice An Article of the Book “Unmask Alice”: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-09-03/true-story-behind-go-ask-alice-diary-of-a-drug-addict-teen/101391508 Psychedelics and Kidneys 1: https://www.asn-online.org/education/kidneyweek/2022/program-abstract.aspx?controlId=3767520 Psychedelics and Kidneys 2: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1080603221001642 The Reward Pathway: https://lpsonline.sas.upenn.edu/features/neuroscience-and-addiction-unraveling-brains-reward-system This is a previous post I did on drug addiction with another extensive sources cited: https://imgur.com/gallery/no-nancy-reagan-addiction-is-not-as-simple-as-just-say-no-qioPQ2D
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: GovBrief Today #536
item.description: Iranian missiles killed 19-year-old Pvt. Isabella Gonzales in Jordan on Friday, one year after she graduated from her Texas high school. 1st Lt. Tyler Feehan, 25, died the next day, weeks from finishing his MBA and set to be married when he came home. The Army named them today and will posthumously promote Feehan to captain. Also today, the Pentagon admitted nearly 100 soldiers have been wounded in two weeks, a number it confirmed only after CNN discovered its casualty database running weeks behind. The government records its dead and honors them. There is no field in the database for a wedding that will never happen. I've emailed 8,308 stories over 536 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260720-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/vet-fedplaza [2] https://go.govbrief.today/netanyahu-arrest [3] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july20 [4] https://go.govbrief.today/energy-website [5] https://go.govbrief.today/canada-fifty [6] https://go.govbrief.today/military-casualties [7] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-threat [8] https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-aid [9] https://go.govbrief.today/nyt-phones [10] https://go.govbrief.today/minnesota-sanctuary [11] https://go.govbrief.today/scientific-manual [12] https://go.govbrief.today/edwards-guns [13] https://go.govbrief.today/jefferson-statue [14] https://go.govbrief.today/california-shutoffs [15] https://go.govbrief.today/paramount-merger Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: LeonSpinks

item.title: 10 books I enjoyed most of the 120 that I've read so far this year.
item.description:
Book two of a good sci-fi series. Group of Scientist kidnapped by an alien race as they try to figure out how to survive under their control.
The world goes insane as aliens arrive on earth. They are here for religious reasons. A lot of horror undertones in this book.
a litrpg with time loop/regressor troupes. I really enjoy the side characters.
Wonderful start to a urban fantasy series. What do you do when an demon fakes your signature on the soul contract?
What do you do when you wake up in your favorite and very deadly fantasy book world? Like what if you woke up in game of thrones world before the wars started?
Matt is fastly becoming my favorite author. I also really loved parade of horribles but this one edged it out just a little bit.
This was a great entry into the Dresden files to me. Seeing the main character deal with the grief he has over all the really bad things that have happened to him.
sci-fi corporate intrigue mixed with an almost John Wick level of badass figure.
Cozy space exploration
Slow burn old west vampires. This has been my favorite book of the year so far.
item.dc:creator: cacuin

item.title: €P§ŧī€Ŋ Đî§ŧṚ☆Č̣ŦÏ○Ɲ
item.description:
All because some very rich people did evil shit and don't want accountability
This cuts Europe off from /any/ shipping not willing to go the long way around the Cape of Good Hope. It impacts the US, China, Australia, the Philippines... basically if you live on Planet Earth this impacts you. Yeah they are targeting Saudi shipping, but nobody worth their salt is going to risk it with LNG tankers in the area.

item.title: I was told after my first colonoscopy that I needed another in 5 years...now they are saying 10. Did someone take my turn?
item.description: They knocked me out with propofol and WOW...when I woke up I felt like I had just had the best sleep EVER despite only being out for about an hour. Makes me understand how MJ got hooked on the stuff.
